skill.name: database-admin
skill.description: Database administration guidance for cloud databases, automation, reliability, performance, security, and disaster recovery.
skill.summary: Plan and operate reliable cloud database platforms with DBA best practices.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported one external command pattern and five weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are false positives in documentation prose. SKILL.md contains database administration guidance only, with no executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection text, or cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Alert Is Benign
low_findings.description: The alert at SKILL.md line 28 refers to opening a local markdown resource for examples. It is an instruction to consult documentation, not Ruby backtick execution or shell command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains a plain markdown resource reference and no executable syntax. No command invocation, shell interpolation, or user-controlled command input is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Alerts Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The alerts at SKILL.md lines 3, 115, 148, 158, and 162 occur in descriptive database administration prose. They do not select, implement, or recommend weak cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain words such as modern, Documentation, and Design in normal prose. No crypto API, cipher choice, hash function, or security-sensitive implementation appears in the file.

content.value_statement: Database platforms require careful planning for reliability, performance, cost, and compliance. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce practical DBA plans, runbooks, and architecture guidance.
actual_capabilities: Designs cloud database architectures across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud services.
Creates high availability, backup, failover, and disaster recovery plans.
Advises on database monitoring, alerting, query analysis, and performance optimization.
Guides database security, access control, encryption, audit logging, and compliance work.
Plans database automation with Terraform, migration tools, GitOps, and CI/CD workflows.
Reviews database cost, capacity, storage, and scaling tradeoffs.
limitations: It provides guidance and plans, not direct access to live databases or cloud consoles.
It needs workload details, compliance needs, and service constraints for precise recommendations.
It cannot verify real backup status, monitoring data, or production configuration without supplied evidence.
Production changes still need review, testing, approvals, and rollback planning by responsible operators.

use_cases.title: Plan a Reliable Cloud Database
use_cases.description: Create a database architecture with replication, backups, failover, observability, and capacity planning.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Production DBA Runbook
use_cases.description: Build operational procedures for maintenance, incident response, recovery testing, and access reviews.

use_cases.title: Improve Database Cost and Performance
use_cases.description: Review workload patterns and identify right-sizing, indexing, connection pooling, and storage improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Describe My Database Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this database environment and list the main reliability, security, backup, and monitoring gaps. Ask for any missing details before recommending changes.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Backup Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a backup and recovery plan for this database workload. Include retention, point-in-time recovery, restore testing, RPO, RTO, monitoring, and ownership.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Performance Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these symptoms and metrics, then propose a safe database performance tuning plan. Include likely causes, validation steps, rollback criteria, and monitoring checks.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Multi-Region Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a multi-region database architecture for this regulated workload. Cover failover, replication, encryption, access control, compliance logging, cost, and disaster recovery tests.

output_examples.input: Design a production PostgreSQL setup for a SaaS application with strict recovery targets.
output: Architecture recommendation with primary, standby, read replica, and backup components.
Recovery targets, restore testing schedule, monitoring alerts, and failover procedure.
Security controls covering access, encryption, audit logging, and credential rotation.

output_examples.input: Review our database maintenance process and identify missing operational controls.
output: Prioritized gap list for backups, patching, monitoring, schema migrations, and access reviews.
Action plan with owner roles, validation steps, maintenance windows, and rollback requirements.
Runbook outline for routine checks and incident response.

output_examples.input: Reduce database cloud spend without reducing reliability.
output: Cost drivers grouped by compute, storage, backup retention, replicas, and data transfer.
Right-sizing and storage recommendations with risk notes and measurement steps.
Change plan that preserves performance targets and recovery objectives.

best_practices: Provide workload size, traffic patterns, recovery targets, compliance requirements, and current platform details.
Ask for validation steps, rollback plans, and monitoring checks before any production database change.
Use the skill to create runbooks and review them with the teams responsible for operations.
anti_patterns: Do not request destructive database commands without backups, approvals, and rollback criteria.
Do not treat generated guidance as proof that backups, replicas, or alerts are working.
Do not share secrets, production credentials, full connection strings, or regulated data in prompts.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to my database?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance based on information you supply. It does not connect to live systems.

faq.question: Can it help with AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud databases?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill covers common managed database services across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud.

faq.question: Can it create disaster recovery plans?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline RPO, RTO, replication, backup, restore testing, failover, and incident procedures.

faq.question: Is it suitable for compliance work?
faq.answer: It can help plan controls for HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOX, GDPR, and audit logging. Final compliance review needs qualified approval.

faq.question: Can it tune query performance?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide query analysis, index review, connection pooling, resource sizing, and monitoring plans.

faq.question: What should I provide for the best results?
faq.answer: Provide database engine, cloud provider, workload pattern, metrics, recovery targets, security requirements, and current pain points.
